---
title: "Continue-on-error"
---

By default a failing workflow aborts the run (fail-fast). With
`bContinueOnError = TRUE`, `RunProject()` records the failure and keeps going,
returning a structured summary of what succeeded and what didn't.

The `demo_continue_on_error/` project has one healthy workflow and one that
calls a function which doesn't exist:

```{.yaml filename="demo_continue_on_error/02_broken/BrokenMetric.yaml"}
steps:
  - name: this_function_does_not_exist
    output: Analysis_Broken
    params:
      x: Raw_Growth
```

```{r}
library(workr)

results <- RunProject(
  strPath          = "demo_continue_on_error",
  lData            = list(Raw_Growth = datasets::ToothGrowth),
  bRecursive       = TRUE,
  bContinueOnError = TRUE
)
```

## Per-workflow status

```{r}
knitr::kable(results$status, caption = "Every workflow, with success/error status")
```

## Just the failures

```{r}
knitr::kable(results$failures, caption = "Failures captured — the run still completed")
```
